# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.

Write secure code.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Secure EC2 instances with encrypted EBS volumes, backup policies, and hardened configuration.

Parameters:
  EnvironmentName:
    Type: String
    Default: production
    AllowedValues:
      - production
      - staging
      - development
    Description: Environment name for tagging and configuration

  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - m5.large
      - m5.xlarge
    Description: EC2 instance type

  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC ID where instances will be launched

  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: Private subnet ID for the EC2 instance

  LatestAmiId:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>
    Default: /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-2023/al2023-ami-kernel-default-x86_64/latest
    Description: Latest Amazon Linux 2023 AMI ID from SSM Parameter Store

  DataVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    MinValue: 20
    MaxValue: 1000
    Description: Size of the application data EBS volume in GB

  AllowedSshCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/8
    AllowedPattern: ^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}/\d{1,2}$
    Description: CIDR block allowed for SSH access (should be internal network only)

  BackupRetentionDays:
    Type: Number
    Default: 30
    MinValue: 7
    MaxValue: 365
    Description: Number of days to retain backups

  EnableDetailedMonitoring:
    Type: String
    Default: 'true'
    AllowedValues:
      - 'true'
      - 'false'
    Description: Enable detailed CloudWatch monitoring

Conditions:
  IsProduction: !Equals [!Ref EnvironmentName, production]
  DetailedMonitoring: !Equals [!Ref EnableDetailedMonitoring, 'true']

Resources:

  # KMS Key for EBS encryption - encryption must not be deferred
  EBSEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: !Sub 'KMS key for EBS volume encryption - ${EnvironmentName}'
      Enabled: true
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: EnableRootAccountAccess
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'
          - Sid: AllowEC2RoleUsage
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !GetAtt EC2InstanceRole.Arn
            Action:
              - kms:Decrypt
              - kms:DescribeKey
              - kms:Encrypt
              - kms:GenerateDataKey
              - kms:GenerateDataKeyWithoutPlaintext
              - kms:ReEncryptFrom
              - kms:ReEncryptTo
            Resource: '*'
          - Sid: AllowAWSBackupUsage
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !GetAtt BackupServiceRole.Arn
            Action:
              - kms:Decrypt
              - kms:DescribeKey
              - kms:Encrypt
              - kms:GenerateDataKey
              - kms:GenerateDataKeyWithoutPlaintext
              - kms:ReEncryptFrom
              - kms:ReEncryptTo
              - kms:CreateGrant
            Resource: '*'
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-ebs-encryption-key'

  EBSEncryptionKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: !Sub 'alias/${EnvironmentName}-ebs-encryption-key'
      TargetKeyId: !Ref EBSEncryptionKey

  # Security Group with minimal required access
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: !Sub 'Security group for ${EnvironmentName} application instances'
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedSshCidr
          Description: HTTPS access from internal network
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: HTTPS outbound for updates and AWS API calls
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: HTTP outbound for package updates
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-sg'

  # IAM Role for EC2 with least privilege
  EC2InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-ec2-instance-role'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/CloudWatchAgentServerPolicy
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EBSVolumeAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ec2:DescribeVolumes
                  - ec2:DescribeTags
                Resource: '*'
        - PolicyName: CloudWatchLogsAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                  - logs:DescribeLogStreams
                Resource: !Sub 'arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/ec2/${EnvironmentName}/*'
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-ec2-instance-profile'
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole

  # CloudWatch Log Group for instance logs
  InstanceLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: !Sub '/ec2/${EnvironmentName}/application'
      RetentionInDays: 90
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  # EC2 Instance with encrypted root and data volumes
  ApplicationInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Metadata:
      AWS::CloudFormation::Init:
        configSets:
          default:
            - setup
            - harden
            - monitoring
        setup:
          packages:
            yum:
              amazon-cloudwatch-agent: []
          commands:
            01_mount_data_volume:
              command: |
                # Wait for the data volume to be available
                while [ ! -e /dev/xvdf ]; do sleep 1; done
                # Check if filesystem exists, create if not
                if ! blkid /dev/xvdf; then
                  mkfs -t xfs /dev/xvdf
                fi
                mkdir -p /data
                mount /dev/xvdf /data
                # Add to fstab for persistence
                echo '/dev/xvdf /data xfs defaults,noatime,nofail 0 2' >> /etc/fstab
                # Set restrictive permissions on data directory
                chmod 750 /data
                chown root:root /data
        harden:
          commands:
            01_disable_unused_filesystems:
              command: |
                echo "install cramfs /bin/true" >> /etc/modprobe.d/disable-filesystems.conf
                echo "install freevxfs /bin/true" >> /etc/modprobe.d/disable-filesystems.conf
                echo "install hfs /bin/true" >> /etc/modprobe.d/disable-filesystems.conf
                echo "install hfsplus /bin/true" >> /etc/modprobe.d/disable-filesystems.conf
                echo "install udf /bin/true" >> /etc/modprobe.d/disable-filesystems.conf
            02_set_sysctl_security:
              command: |
                cat >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-security.conf << 'EOF'
                net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
                net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
                net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
                net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
                net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1
                net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1
                net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1
                net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1
                net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1
                net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1
                net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
                net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
                EOF
                sysctl --system
            03_configure_ssh_hardening:
              command: |
                sed -i 's/#PermitRootLogin yes/PermitRootLogin no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
                sed -i 's/#MaxAuthTries 6/MaxAuthTries 3/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
                sed -i 's/#PermitEmptyPasswords no/PermitEmptyPasswords no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
                sed -i 's/X11Forwarding yes/X11Forwarding no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
                echo "ClientAliveInterval 300" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
                echo "ClientAliveCountMax 2" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
                echo "Protocol 2" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
                systemctl restart sshd
        monitoring:
          files:
            /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json:
              content: !Sub |
                {
                  "agent": {
                    "metrics_collection_interval": 60,
                    "run_as_user": "cwagent"
                  },
                  "logs": {
                    "logs_collected": {
                      "files": {
                        "collect_list": [
                          {
                            "file_path": "/var/log/messages",
                            "log_group_name": "/ec2/${EnvironmentName}/application",
                            "log_stream_name": "{instance_id}/messages",
                            "retention_in_days": 90
                          },
                          {
                            "file_path": "/var/log/secure",
                            "log_group_name": "/ec2/${EnvironmentName}/application",
                            "log_stream_name": "{instance_id}/secure",
                            "retention_in_days": 90
                          }
                        ]
                      }
                    }
                  },
                  "metrics": {
                    "namespace": "${EnvironmentName}/EC2",
                    "metrics_collected": {
                      "disk": {
                        "measurement": ["used_percent", "inodes_free"],
                        "resources": ["*"]
                      },
                      "mem": {
                        "measurement": ["mem_used_percent"]
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              mode: '000644'
              owner: root
              group: root
          commands:
            01_start_cloudwatch_agent:
              command: |
                /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl \
                  -a fetch-config -m ec2 \
                  -c file:/opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json -s
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref LatestAmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      Monitoring: !If [DetailedMonitoring, true, false]
      # IMDSv2 required - prevents SSRF attacks
      MetadataOptions:
        HttpTokens: required
        HttpPutResponseHopLimit: 1
        HttpEndpoint: enabled
      # Encrypted root volume
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: 30
            VolumeType: gp3
            Encrypted: true
            KmsKeyId: !GetAtt EBSEncryptionKey.Arn
            DeleteOnTermination: true
        # Encrypted application data volume
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvdf
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref DataVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            Encrypted: true
            KmsKeyId: !GetAtt EBSEncryptionKey.Arn
            DeleteOnTermination: false
            Iops: 3000
            Throughput: 125
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash -xe
          # Update system packages
          yum update -y --security
          # Install cfn-bootstrap
          yum install -y aws-cfn-bootstrap
          # Run cfn-init
          /opt/aws/bin/cfn-init -v \
            --stack ${AWS::StackName} \
            --resource ApplicationInstance \
            --configsets default \
            --region ${AWS::Region}
          # Signal completion
          /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal -e $? \
            --stack ${AWS::StackName} \
            --resource ApplicationInstance \
            --region ${AWS::Region}
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-app-instance'
        - Key: Backup
          Value: 'true'
        - Key: DataClassification
          Value: confidential
    CreationPolicy:
      ResourceSignal:
        Timeout: PT15M

  # AWS Backup - backup policies included from the start, not deferred
  BackupServiceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-backup-service-role'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: backup.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSBackupServiceRolePolicyForBackup
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSBackupServiceRolePolicyForRestores
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  BackupVault:
    Type: AWS::Backup::BackupVault
    Properties:
      BackupVaultName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-backup-vault'
      EncryptionKeyArn: !GetAtt EBSEncryptionKey.Arn
      AccessPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: DenyDeleteRecoveryPoints
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action:
              - backup:DeleteRecoveryPoint
              - backup:UpdateRecoveryPointLifecycle
            Resource: '*'

  BackupPlan:
    Type: AWS::Backup::BackupPlan
    Properties:
      BackupPlan:
        BackupPlanName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-daily-backup-plan'
        BackupPlanRule:
          - RuleName: DailyBackup
            TargetBackupVault: !Ref BackupVault
            ScheduleExpression: 'cron(0 3 * * ? *)'
            StartWindowMinutes: 60
            CompletionWindowMinutes: 180
            Lifecycle:
              DeleteAfterDays: !Ref BackupRetentionDays
            EnableContinuousBackup: false
          - !If
            - IsProduction
            - RuleName: WeeklyBackup
              TargetBackupVault: !Ref BackupVault
              ScheduleExpression: 'cron(0 5 ? * SUN *)'
              StartWindowMinutes: 60
              CompletionWindowMinutes: 360
              Lifecycle:
                DeleteAfterDays: 90
              EnableContinuousBackup: false
            - !Ref AWS::NoValue

  BackupSelection:
    Type: AWS::Backup::BackupSelection
    Properties:
      BackupPlanId: !Ref BackupPlan
      BackupSelection:
        SelectionName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-ec2-backup-selection'
        IamRoleArn: !GetAtt BackupServiceRole.Arn
        ListOfTags:
          - ConditionType: STRINGEQUALS
            ConditionKey: Backup
            ConditionValue: 'true'

  # CloudWatch Alarms for monitoring
  HighCPUAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-high-cpu-utilization'
      AlarmDescription: Alarm when CPU exceeds 80% for 5 minutes
      MetricName: CPUUtilization
      Namespace: AWS/EC2
      Statistic: Average
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 2
      Threshold: 80
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: InstanceId
          Value: !Ref ApplicationInstance
      TreatMissingData: breaching

  StatusCheckAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub '${EnvironmentName}-instance-status-check'
      AlarmDescription: Alarm when instance status check fails
      MetricName: StatusCheckFailed
      Namespace: AWS/EC2
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 2
      Threshold: 0
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: InstanceId
          Value: !Ref ApplicationInstance
      TreatMissingData: breaching

  # VPC Flow Logs for network monitoring (if not already enabled at VPC level)
  InstanceENIFlowLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: !Sub '/vpc/flowlogs/${EnvironmentName}/instance-eni'
      RetentionInDays: 90

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: EC2 Instance ID
    Value: !Ref ApplicationInstance

  PrivateIpAddress:
    Description: Private IP address of the instance
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationInstance.PrivateIp

  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: Security Group ID
    Value: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup

  KMSKeyArn:
    Description: KMS Key ARN used for EBS encryption
    Value: !GetAtt EBSEncryptionKey.Arn

  BackupVaultArn:
    Description: Backup Vault ARN
    Value: !GetAtt BackupVault.BackupVaultArn

  BackupPlanId:
    Description: Backup Plan ID
    Value: !Ref BackupPlan

  IAMRoleArn:
    Description: EC2 Instance IAM Role ARN
    Value: !GetAtt EC2InstanceRole.Arn
